title: quercus stellata subsp. margaretta
intro: Quercus stellata subsp. margaretta, also known as the Texas post oak, is a subspecies of oak tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small leaves and distinctive star-shaped bark. The medicinal properties of Quercus stellata subsp. margaretta include its high tannin content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in reducing fever, relieving coughs, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Quercus stellata subsp. margaretta include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often used to create teas, salves, and other topical applications.
